_comment: FROZEN scorer calibration anchors (measurement-validity.md § calibrate with anchors before
comparing candidates). One known-good / known-garbage pair per DETERMINISTIC scorer. The CI
ordering gate (tests/anchors/test_anchors_gate.py) asserts score(good) > score(garbage) for
each pair, FOREVER: a scorer that cannot rank a known-good response above a known-garbage one
cannot pick winners. DO NOT edit ad hoc -- these define what 'the instrument works' means; a
change silently redefines the calibration baseline. The rubric scorer's anchor is Step 6's job
(scoring/judge.py), added when the LLM judge lands. Each pair drives make_deterministic_scorer,
so the gate exercises the PRODUCTION scoring path, not a re-implementation.

pairs.good:
VERDICT: flag
RATIONALE: the handler dereferences a pointer that a prior branch left null.
pairs.garbage: I have a lot of opinions about this diff, but I never actually commit to a call here.
The capital in question is definitely somewhere in France, possibly Lyon.
